key_insight: HACO used sparse human guidance to transfer a masked generative modeling idea from vision into crystal structure prediction, producing MaskGXT.
engineering_relevance: The work suggests that AI co-scientists can accelerate materials algorithm design by recombining transferable generative methods with domain-specific crystallography constraints.
method: Searches across generative modeling approaches in multiple fields to find a transferable CSP framework.
Instantiates MaskGIT as a discrete token model for crystal structures.
Adds symmetry tokens, space-group-stratified sampling, and sub-bin coordinate refinement to improve polymorph coverage and accuracy.
evidence_signal: On the MP-20 polymorph split, MaskGXT achieved 79.06% METRe accuracy versus 70.87% for the strongest baseline, and it also led on standard MP-20 and MPTS-52 benchmarks.
watch_item: Results are strongest in settings with fast, cheap, well-aligned validation, so broader real-world CSP transfer may depend on the quality of domain constraints and evaluation.
paper_title: Discovering Crystal Structure Prediction Algorithms with an AI Co-Scientist
